items.title: Wall Street Week | Hubbard on Fed Cut Fallout, Open Source AI, Nuclear Bet, Department Store Revival
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>This week, Glenn Hubbard warns that tariffs, shaky data and a mature credit cycle create risks as the Fed looks toward 2026. And, will open AI ecosystems win out over closed models, as AMD CEO Lisa Su and former IBM CEO Sam Palmisano suggest? Plus, from AI to manufacturing, soaring electricity demand is forcing a rethink of where our power comes from and how fast we can build to generate it. Later, can Macy’s reinvent the department store for today’s shopper?

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Glenn Hubbard on The Fed
00:09:54 - Open Source AI
00:23:48 - Betting on Nuclear
00:35:41 - Macy's Comeback
00:47:27 - Lisa Su on AI
--------

items.title: EU-Russia, Epstein photos and a slightly drier holiday
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The EU agrees to indefinitely freeze Russian assets, removing obstacles for a loan to Ukraine. The admiral leading U.S. troops in Latin America steps down. Trump appears in newly released Epstein photos. Plus, Americans say they’re drinking less, but new data shows the drop is only about one drink per week.

items.title: Why Open Source AI Could Be the Best Bet for Developers and Investors
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:

AMD chair and CEO Lisa Su and former IBM CEO Sam Palmisano explain why that choice could shape the next era of computing.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>These Hungarian metalworkers are stripping half-naked for an annual charity calendar. Dressed only in his protective work apron, baring his tattoos and beer belly, metalworker Janos Bocs poses for the camera while an industrial fan — usually used to cool red-hot metal — blows his long hair.

items.title: Trump's Executive Orders: Are They Legal? #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Executive orders, from George Washington's first directive on, have been pivotal throughout US history. However, President Trump has used executive orders to advance his agenda in a more aggressive way than any president before him, prompting, at times, legal challenges and judicial intervention.</article>

items.title: ASML CEO: China Won't Accept Being Cut Off From AI Chips
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Christophe Fouquet, the CEO of ASML, tells Bloomberg Television that China will not accept being cutoff from Western chips, artificial intelligence and technology. ASML is the only company in the world making extreme ultraviolet lithography machines, which are vital for manufacturing advanced AI chips used by the likes of Nvidia and Apple. Fouquet spoke with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie in an exclusive conversation at the company's headquarters in Veldhoven, the Netherlands, on November 14.

Watch the full episode: https://youtu.be/NZCRAg6rais

00:00 Who wins the AI Race?
00:45 Europe's 'weak' AI industry
02:00 Can Europe compete on AI?
02:59 Rare earths: Is Europe dependent on China?
03:43 Competition with China

--------

items.title: How the U.S. Army 3-D Prints Lethal Drones in Hours | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Pentagon is testing portable 3D-printing labs in Hawaii that let U.S. soldiers design, print and assemble FPV drones in a matter of hours—turning vulnerable supply lines into on-demand battlefield factories. The ability to produce these user-guided munitions quickly is seen as crucial in any Pacific conflict, where logistics could be easily disrupted.

WSJ joined the U.S. military on Oahu to see how troops are preparing for a new era of war dominated by drones.

Chapters:
0:00 On the ground in Oahu
0:26 The lab
1:41 Why printing drones is important 
2:07 Prepping for drone warfare 
3:30 Strategy and testing the drones 
5:05 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Drones #Geopolitics #WSJ</article>

items.title: Will Trump's huge gamble on letting Nvidia sell AI chips to #China pay off? #tech #ai #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>On this week’s episode of Everybody’s Business, Nilay Patel, editor in chief of the Verge, joins Max Chafkin and Stacey Vanek Smith to unpack President Donald Trump’s decision to lift export restrictions on AI chips made by Nvidia, AMD and Intel and why it may not be enough to stop the AI bubble from bursting https://link.podtrac.com/ujb7ns2p

This week we take a look at Trump's latest AI gambit: letting Nvidia sell their AI chips to China. Will this move help feed AI's growth or is it just another step towards making the bubble all the more explosive when it pops? The Verge's Nilay Patel joins Max and Stacey to get to the bottom of this. Also on this show, Lucas Shaw goes through the ins-and-outs of the complicated fight over Warner Bros. Will Netflix succeed in their bid for the storied media giant, or will it be Paramount with its attempt at a hostile takeover? See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information</article>

items.title: Trump Leaning Between Kevin Warsh, Kevin Hassett for Fed Chair | Balance of Power 12/12/2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Rep. Jay Obernolte (R-CA) discusses President Trump's executive order signing and Congress in a deadlock over the health care plan as the deadline looms. Miriam Vogel, Equal AI President & CEO discusses President Trump signing an executive order to limit state-level regulations. Hagar Chemali, Greenwich Media Strategies Founder & CEO, discusses the latest progress between US, Ukraine & Russia.

items.title: The Fed’s Next Chair Faces AI Uncertainty, Political Heat and Credit Risks
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The Federal Reserve has just cut rates by 25 basis points into an economy it still expects to grow faster next year, raising questions about why it eased and how much further it can go. Former Council of Economic Advisers chair Glenn Hubbard discusses the decision, the neutral rate and why he doesn’t see much more room for rate cuts going into 2026.

items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:

items.title: Trump Says He Should Have a Role in Talking to the Fed on Rates
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump says he thinks his "voice should be heard" when it comes to making recommendations on the interest rates set by the Federal Reserve. He speaks with reporters in the Oval Office. 

Sign up for the Washington Edition newsletter to find out how the worlds of money and politics intersect in the US capital: https://bloom.bg/3KkEimO

items.title: US admiral leading US troops in Latin America steps down | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Admiral Alvin Holsey, head of US military forces in Latin America, retired two years early amid rising tensions with Venezuela and recent strikes on suspected drug-smuggling boats.

#unitedstates #venezuela #News #Reuters #Newsfeed
